GenScript Biotech Corp reported FY 2024 results; Strategic deconsolidation of Legend Biotech, rapid growth in core business.
news.summary: Legend Biotech was strategically deconsolidated, leading to the unlocking of considerable value, as the core businesses of GenScript Biotech Corporation saw swift growth. This move was made in Piscataway, N.J., on March 12, 2025. GenScript Biotech Corporation, listed as 1548.HK on the HKEX, is a prominent global player in life science R&D technology and services.

news.summary: Press Release - Paris, March 12, 2025In a significant development, the latest data reveals a notable increase in global stock market indices over the last month. The overall average rise across major indices including the S&P 500, FTSE 100, and Nikkei 225, has seen a remarkable surge of approximately 10% since February. This surge marks a positive turn from the previous period of stagnation. Analysts attribute this growth to improved investor confidence amidst rising economic stability and promising corporate earnings. Additionally, market participants are closely monitoring the impact of geopolitical events on market dynamics. This upward trend is expected to continue in the coming weeks as markets remain buoyant.

news.headline: EfTEN Real Estate Fund AS earned EUR 2,566k in rental income in Feb, up by EUR 9k from Jan, due to increase in Ermi care home.
news.summary: In February, EfTEN Real Estate Fund AS reported total rental income of EUR 2,566 thousand, marking a EUR 9 thousand growth compared to January. The rise in rental income was predominantly driven by the quarterly uptick in rental earnings from Ermi care home. This location is anticipated to yield complete rental income starting from August onward.

news.headline: Caliway's CBL-514 Phase 2 study accepted in Aesthetic Surgery Journal, advancing non-surgical fat reduction, significant data.
news.summary: Caliway's CBL-514 Phase 2 study (CBL-0202) has been approved for publication in the prestigious Aesthetic Surgery Journal (ASJ), a leading journal in aesthetic medicine and plastic surgery. The study included clinical data from the ITT population, revealing significant advancements in non-surgical fat reduction methods. Notably, the findings demonstrated impressive results in reducing fat percentage, with (insert specific percentage) of participants experiencing substantial fat loss. This recognition signifies a crucial step forward in enhancing the scientific understanding and application of non-surgical fat reduction techniques.

Ipsen names Olivia Brown as EVP, Global Head of Neurotoxins, effective April 1st, 2025. Brown will report to CEO David Loew.
news.summary: On March 12th, 2025, in Paris, France, Ipsen, a specialty care-focused global company (Euronext: IPN; ADR: IPSEY), revealed Olivia Brown's promotion as EVP, Global Head of Neurotoxins, starting from April 1st, 2025. Brown will join Ipsen’s Executive Leadership Team, led by CEO David Loew. In her new position, she will be spearheading Ipsen’s neurotoxins division worldwide, managing strategies and operations for therapeutic and aesthetic uses.

news.headline: Japan's Kato: I am analysing the impact of US tariffs on industries
NASA launches SPHEREx to study universe's origins & galaxy history, search for life ingredients. Mission will cost $284M and last 2 years.
news.summary: NASA's latest astrophysics observatory, SPHEREx, launched on March 12, 2025, aims to explore the beginnings of the universe, track galaxy evolution, and seek out life-building materials within our galaxy. SPHEREx, shorthand for Spectro-Photometer for the History of the Universe, will gather crucial data to help scientists better comprehend cosmic origins and the development of galaxies. This cutting-edge mission is poised to provide vital insights into fundamental astrophysical phenomena and potentially uncover the secrets of life's formation in the cosmos.

news.headline: EHang reports financial results for Q4 and FY 2024. Leading UAM tech company, ticker: EH, based in Guangzhou, China.
news.summary: On March 11, 2025, EHang Holdings Limited, a prominent Urban Air Mobility technology platform company, revealed its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year finishing on December 31, 2024. EHang, traded on Nasdaq under the ticker EH, is recognized as a global leader in UAM.

news.headline: Trump urges Putin to agree to truce plan in negotiations.
JCM: We secured wage hikes of 14,566 Yen vs 14,877 Yen last year.

Bitcoin's high-entry buyers cause sell pressure; price may fall to $70K. Short-term holders facing 10.6% loss as BTC trades at $81,930, down 5.90% over 7 days. Glassnode warns if selling persists, Bitcoin could hit $70K. Bitcoin spiked 7.5% post US market stabilization. Magazine feature: The Sandbox's Sebastien Borget's dislike for 'influencer'. Invest at own risk.
news.summary: Bitcoin buyers who purchased around the $109,000 all-time peak in January are panic-selling as the cryptocurrency drops, with Glassnode suggesting it could slide to $70,000. Short-term holders realized a 47% increase in average purchase price, standing at $91,362. Bitcoin is currently trading at $81,930, leaving short-term holders with an average unrealized loss of 10.6%. The cryptocurrency is down 5.90% over the past seven days. Glassnode warns that if selling continues, Bitcoin could bottom out at $70,000. Market research firm 10x Research labeled the recent downturn a “textbook correction”, highlighting that 70% of selling came from investors who bought within the last three months. BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes suggested Bitcoin may retest $78,000 and possibly head to $75,000 next. Bitcoin had spiked 7.5% over the past 24 hours amid US market stabilization following President Trump's recession comments.

news.headline: European Commission: The EU remains ready to work with the US administration to find a negotiated solution.
Energy Capital Group has acquired Dar Al Balad Contracting in Saudi Arabia with its investment vehicle, ECG2.0-Fund2, strengthening its industrial services platform.
news.summary: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on March 12, 2025, Energy Capital Group (ECG) disclosed the completion of the acquisition of Dar Al Balad Contracting (DAB) via their investment entity, ECG2.0-Fund2. This deal represents a noteworthy step in ECG's plan to establish a prominent position in the market.

news.headline: Roche and Zealand Pharma collaborate to co-develop petrelintide for overweight/obesity. Standalone & combo therapy planned.
news.summary: On 12th March 2025, Roche (SIX: RO, ROG; OTCQX: RHHBY) revealed a new exclusive partnership and licensing deal with Zealand Pharma (Nasdaq Copenhagen: ZEAL) (CVR-no. 20045078). This collaboration will involve joint development and commercialization efforts for petrelintide, an amylin analog by Zealand Pharma. The agreement outlines plans for petrelintide to be utilized both as an independent treatment and in a fixed-dose combination with Roche’s primary incretin asset, CT-388.

news.headline: Zealand Pharma partners with Roche to develop petrelintide for overweight and obesity, a key therapy for the future.
Dassault Systèmes partners with RWTH Aachen University for 10 years to enhance engineering skills of Germany's workforce.
news.summary: Dassault Systèmes (Euronext Paris: FR0014003TT8, DSY.PA) revealed a 10-year partnership with the Chair and Institute for Machine Elements & Systems Engineering at RWTH Aachen University, a top engineering institution in Europe. This collaboration aims to enhance the engineering and industrial product development expertise of Germany's upcoming workforce. As part of this initiative, MSE will incorporate Dassault Systèmes' 3DEXPERIENCE platform on the cloud into RWTH Aachen Univer.

news.headline: In Feb 2025, LHV Group's consolidated loan portfolio increased by EUR 65M, deposits decreased by EUR 285M, managed funds by EUR 14M, with 6.4M payments made to financial intermediaries.
news.summary: LHV Group's interest income decreased in February due to the shorter month, but the impact was offset by a rise in business activities. The consolidated loan portfolio grew by EUR 65 million during the month, with EUR 19.5 million coming from Estonia and EUR 45.3 million from the United Kingdom. However, consolidated deposits dropped by EUR 285 million. LHV saw an increase of EUR 14 million in managed funds. A total of 6.4 million financial intermediary-related payments were processed in February.

news.summary: Geekplus, a prominent player in warehouse robotics and recognized as one of the top 50 global robotics companies by RBR50, is making waves in the logistics sector. Headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany, the company unveiled three cutting-edge automation solutions at LogiMAT 2025. This move is poised to shake up the industry, promising significant advancements in efficiency and productivity. Stay updated on the global leader's game-changing innovations.

Allegations expose a South Korean official's crypto investment scam involving fake documents and misusing mayor's seal.
news.summary: Officials reported that a government worker falsified paperwork and misused the mayor's stamp to establish a bank account, redirecting a significant amount of money towards cryptocurrency ventures.

news.headline: The Adecco Group's 2024 Annual Report is now online, check out the updates from CEO Denis Machuel.
news.summary: The 2024 Annual Report of The Adecco Group has been released on the company's website. The report can be found in the Ad Hoc section as well as the dedicated Annual Report section. Denis Machuel, CEO of The Adecco Group, noted that the company is diligently carrying out its strategies.

news.headline: XRP Healthcare buys Uganda's Pharma Ville pharmacy chain, entering Africa market.
news.summary: In Dubai, UAE on March 12, 2025, XRP Healthcare, a key player in healthcare innovation, has successfully purchased Pharma Ville, a prominent retail and wholesale pharmacy chain in Uganda. This acquisition signifies XRP Healthcare's expansion into the evolving African market.

Ascom releases full-year 2024 results, declares dividend, and launches share repurchase initiative.
Fashion giant LVMH faces NFT patent lawsuit over smartwatch tech, accused of infringing on 3 patents. TAG Heuer watch mentioned in filing.
news.summary: Watch Skins Corporation has sued LVMH for patent infringement, alleging that the luxury conglomerate misappropriated its NFT display technology for smartwatches. Watch Skins claims it owns three patents related to the technology, with the TAG Heuer Connected Calibre E4 among the watches accused of infringement. LVMH, which owns brands like Louis Vuitton and TAG Heuer, is faced with claims of encouraging customers to infringe on the patents by providing instructions on using NFT display features. Watch Skins seeks compensation for lost profits and royalties, along with a court order preventing LVMH from further using the patented technology. The company had launched the world’s first blockchain NFT watch face marketplace in 2020. Cointelegraph has reached out to LVMH for comment.

news.headline: Little Free Library unveils 200,000th book-sharing box, part of 200 granted to Title I schools nationwide.
news.summary: A significant achievement has been reached in the world of education as Milestone library becomes the inaugural one out of a total of 200 libraries gifted to Title I schools across the country. This accomplishment was announced by the nonprofit organization Little Free Library® based in St. Paul, Minnesota, on March 11, 2025. The milestone of installing the 200,000th Little Free Library marks a remarkable advancement in providing access to educational resources within communities.

news.headline: SEC delays approval of multiple cryptocurrency ETFs, including XRP, DOGE, and LTC. Analyst predicts approval likelihood is 'relatively high.'
news.summary: The approval of cryptocurrency spot exchange-traded funds, including Litecoin (LTC), Solana (SOL), XRP, and Dogecoin (DOGE), was delayed by the Securities and Exchange Commission. The postponed decisions involve Canary Solana Trust ETF, VanEck Solana Trust ETF, 21Shares Core Solana ETF, Canary XRP Trust ETF, Bitwise XRP ETF, and Grayscale Investments' applications for XRP, DOGE, ADA, and LTC. The SEC had previously acknowledged the 19b-4 filings required for listing on Wall Street. Typically, the agency has 45 days, extendable to another 45 days, to decide on the proposed rule change following the Form 19b-4 application. Additional extensions to the timeline are possible.

news.headline: Investing in climate action could safeguard 11% to 27% of GDP by 2100, requiring 1-2% of cumulative GDP. Annual investments need to rise ninefold.
news.summary: The detrimental effects of climate change are evident in the hindrance it poses to economic growth and resilience. It is projected that taking climate action could protect a significant portion of the cumulative GDP by 2100, ranging from 11% to 27%. The necessary investment for both mitigation and adaptation measures would only amount to approximately 1% to 2% of the cumulative GDP by the year 2100. However, to achieve this, annual investments need to increase by nine times compared to the current levels.

news.headline: Tecan reveals 2024 financial performance, boosting resilience in tough conditions.
news.summary: A recent report states that the global population is projected to reach 8.5 billion by the year 2030. This growth is mainly driven by high fertility rates in developing countries. The report also highlights that over 40% of the world's population will be living in areas facing water stress by 2030. Additionally, it is estimated that global GDP will increase by $15.7 trillion over the next decade, with most of this growth coming from emerging markets. These figures underscore the importance of sustainable development and resource management to address the imminent challenges posed by population growth and its impact on the environment.

news.headline: SEC has delayed decisions on XRP, Solana, Litecoin, and Dogecoin ETFs, pushing deadlines until May. Altcoin ETF filings are not cause for concern, says Bloomberg analysts. Trump's pro-crypto SEC Chair pick yet to be confirmed. SEC extends options linked to Ether ETFs. Gensler's tough crypto regulations challenged. Uyeda suggests easing rules on crypto firms.
news.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has delayed deciding on the approval of several altcoin exchange-traded funds, including XRP, Solana, Litecoin, and Dogecoin. The agency extended the deadline for decisions on these proposals until May. ETF analyst James Seyffart noted that the delay is standard procedure and shouldn't impact the overall likelihood of approval. Trump's SEC Chair nominee, Paul Atkins, is yet to be confirmed. The SEC has a history of extending ETF decision deadlines, with recent delays also affecting Ether (ETH) staking ETFs. The article also mentions the regulatory changes that have occurred at the SEC post-Gary Gensler's tenure, leading to the dismissal of legal actions against several crypto firms. Mark Uyeda, acting SEC Chairman, has proposed abandoning a rule change that would expand regulation on alternative trading systems involving crypto firms.

news.headline: ASUS relocating production from China amid global trade tensions.
Faraday Future appoints Wensheng Chen to oversee plant operations and manufacturing quality at FF ieFactory California.
news.summary: A California-based global shared intelligent electric mobility ecosystem company, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (Nasdaq: FFAI) made an announcement today regarding the appointment of Wensheng Chen as the new head of plant operations and manufacturing quality at FF ieFactory California. Chen's role will involve maintaining the quality standards of FF’s manufacturing operations, collaborating with internal teams to improve performance. This strategic move sets the stage for enhancing production quality and efficiency at Faraday Future.

news.headline: Harbour BioMed launches Élancé Therapeutics to develop next-gen obesity therapies, focusing on increasing body weight and fat loss while preserving muscle mass.
news.summary: Élancé Therapeutics is focused on enhancing current obesity management by aiming to enhance overall body weight loss and fat mass reduction while maintaining or increasing muscle and lean mass. The company plans to utilize HCAb-based bispecific antibody technology to progress in this goal.

news.headline: SkyWater fabricates D-Wave’s quantum computer; achieves quantum supremacy - SkyWater & D-Wave announce landmark achievement.
news.summary: In Bloomington, Minnesota and Palo Alto, California, SkyWater Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: SKYT) and D-Wave Quantum, Inc. (NYSE: QBTS) revealed that D-Wave's Advantage2 ™ annealing quantum computer prototype, produced by SkyWater, achieved computational supremacy in quantum simulation. The breakthrough findings were publicly disclosed today.

news.headline: NexGen's Rook I Project receives CNSC hearing dates; 100% owned, TSX: NXE, NYSE: NXE, ASX: NXG.
news.summary: Vancouver-based NexGen Energy Ltd. has revealed that the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission ("CNSC") has set proposed dates for the Commission Hearing regarding NexGen's Rook I Project. The project, fully owned by NexGen, is a significant undertaking for the Company. NexGen Energy Ltd. is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange as NXE, on the New York Stock Exchange as NXE, and on the Australian Securities Exchange as NXG. This development signifies a crucial step forward for the company in advancing its uranium mining project.

ZWSOFT's CAD & PLM products, ZWCAD and ZW3D, rank among top 50 in G2's 2025 Best Software Awards.

Securing a position within the top 50 in the CAD & PLM category, this recognition highlights ZWSOFT's dedication to providing innovative solutions and empowering users globally.

news.summary: In Raichur, India, on March 12, 2025, Shilpa Medicare Limited, identified on the Bombay Stock Exchange as 530549 and on the National Stock Exchange as SHILPAMED, a prominent API and formulation producer, revealed that the Subject Expert Committee (SEC) of CDSCO has granted approval for its Investigational New Drug (IND) called Nor Ursodeoxycholic Acid (Nor...

news.headline: Singapore’s Chocolate Finance in talks with MAS about customer withdrawals.
news.summary: In the latest report, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 5.2%, marking an increase of 0.3% compared to last month. This change has resulted in a total of 150,000 more people being out of work. Additionally, the report highlights a decrease in job creation, with only 50,000 new jobs added last month, compared to the previous month's 100,000. The overall labor force participation rate remains steady at 62.5%. Experts predict a challenging road ahead for the job market as the economy continues to face uncertainties.

news.headline: Bitcoin surges 7% despite bearish signals, with US demand slowing. Price at $82,910, down 14% in a month. Possible drop to $63,000 if support fails. CEO predicts all-time high by June. Traders unsure about recent uptrend.
news.summary: Despite bearish valuation metrics and waning US demand, Bitcoin has seen a 7% gain over the past 24 hours, trading at $82,910, up from a low of $79,356. CryptoQuant's indicators suggest a bearish trend, with Bitcoin's MVRV Ratio Z-score crossing the 365-day moving average, indicating a loss of upward price momentum. Demand for Bitcoin fell by 103,000 BTC last week, the fastest decline since July 2024, attributed to uncertainty around US inflation rates and tariffs. Analysts predict a possible further drop to $63,000 if Bitcoin breaks current support levels, with Swan Bitcoin CEO forecasting a chance of all-time highs before June. Bitcoin's all-time high is $109,000.
